title: RoutingGraph
description: Input graph for Module C offline tactical routing. Nodes are map positions with grid coordinates; edges are directional connections with terrain-aware cost multipliers. The worker computes Dijkstra/A* over this structure — do not pass pre-computed paths.

nodes.description: All navigable positions. gridX/gridY are integer pixel-space grid coordinates used for A* heuristic distance. lat/lon are WGS-84 degrees (signed float) for display only.

status.description: clear = passable; damaged = passable with cost penalty; blocked = near-impassable (high cost, not zero)

edges.description: Directed edges. Cost = euclidean grid distance × terrain weight for the destination node status × vehicle weight modifier.

baseCost.description: Optional override for base traversal cost. If omitted, use euclidean distance between gridX/gridY of from and to nodes.

mode.description: Vehicle mode determines terrain weight multipliers. infantry: blocked×100, damaged×50, clear×1. armored: blocked×10, damaged×1, clear×1.

startId.description: Node id of the route start point.

endId.description: Node id of the route end point.

_terrainWeights.description: Canonical weight table — do not modify without flagging to orchestrator and updating agent-routing.md.
